entry.title: Format setting for react/jsx-tag-spacing with Web Storm
entry.content: Settings | Editor | Code Style | HTML | Other => In empty tag

entry.title: Change the destination of Mac screenshots
entry.content:
1. Create a directory

e.g. Create Screenshots directory on Desktop

2. Set the directory as the destination

Tips

ActionCommand
Entire screen[command]+[shift]+[3]
Selected range[command]+[shift]+[4]
Only selected window[command]+[shift]+[space]+[4]
Display control[command]+[shift]+[5]
Copy to clipboard[command]+[shift]+[control]+[3/4]

entry.title: Notes for React
entry.content:

㊙️ Fix hot reload

As of the current version(React “^17.0.1”), the hot reloading is not working

  1. Create a file named .env in the root directory(same level with package.json file)
  2. Add this line and save

㊙️ Create store with Saga and ReduxDevtools

entry.title: Javascript Array.reduce() sounds awesome
entry.content:
Basic

Roughly speaking, it is a function that returns single value from multiple element (arrays and objects)

🤯 🤦 😱 😵 💀 💩…

ℹ️ Basically, it seems to be better to set the initial value, because executing an empty array without an initial value may result in an error or unexpected execution result.
–> https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/Reduce

🍙 Example 1: Calculate total

Let’s calculate total of array.

🤔 🤔 🤔…

OK… but make it a bit easier to read…

🤔… 👌…

🍙 Example 2: Get a max number

🍙 Example 3: Remove value from array of element

e.g. Remove id and create a new array of elements with name key and types key

🍙 Example 4: Find Best/Worst Subject and calculate total score

Well… it could be done better 🤔. I will update the logic later sometime.

🍙 Refs:

  • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/Reduce

entry.title: How to sum up an array of integers with javascript
entry.content:
fa? js does not have sum method for array!?

Javascript does not have Array.prototype.sum() actually but js can calculate with;

  • for loop
  • for in loop
  • forEach loop
  • map loop
  • reduce loop

♻️ for loop

♻️ for in loop

♻️ forEach loop

♻️ map loop

♻️ reduce loop

Summary

  • In terms of the performance, for and reduce seems very good performance 🏆
    https://jsben.ch/MlsMo
  • Readability: reduce looks the simplest 🏆

==> 👑 reduce

entry.title: Debug node.js with VS Code
entry.content:
Set up VS Code to debug node.js

🚗 1. Add js files

🚗 2. Create a launch.jsonfile

🚗 3. Edit launch.json to set the entry point

In this case, main.js is the entry point.

🚗 4. Set breakpoint and Run

Yay! easy ✨

💰💰💰 Bonus! - test with Jest 💰💰💰

🚙 1. Initialise npm

🚙 2. Install Jest

🚙 3. Add test sctipt to package.json

🚙 4. Create test file

🚙 5. Run test

Yay! Easy as 🎉🎉🎉

entry.title: Javascrip nested object destructuring
entry.content:
Destructuring nested object

We can destroy not only flat objects but also nested objects 👍

Ah it’s easy 🏆😄🥇

entry.title: Remove global npm package
entry.content:
Remove global npm package

e.g. Remove create-react-app npm package

  1. npm uninstall -g create-react-app
  2. npm cache clean --force
  3. npm cache verify
  4. which create-react-app
  5. If you got something like /usr/local/bin/create-react-app
  6. rm -rf /usr/local/bin/create-react-app

entry.title: Terminal command and Create aliases
entry.content:
⛳ Terminal Command

  • pwd: Print Working Directory
  • cd ~: Move to home directory
  • cd: Move to home directory
  • command + k: Clear
  • nano <file_name>: Edit with nano
  • rm -r <directory_name>: -r is recursive option

⛳ Create Aliases commands

  1. cd ~
  2. sudo nano .bash_profile
  3. alias ys=‘yarn start’ —> Save and Exit
  4. Restart terminal
  5. ys —> Happy💥

⛳ Show all aliases list

  1. cd
  2. alias

⛳ Refereces

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0liXeoADU6A

🤔 Can’t open VS Code form Terminal !?

If you got this error: -bash: code: command not found

  1. Launch VS Code
  2. Cmd + Shift + P
  3. Type Shell —> Shell Command: Install 'code' command in PATH command
  4. Restart terminal

entry.title: Getting started with Cypress - E2E Testing Framework
entry.content:
👑 Goal

Set up Cypress with React App

⚔️ Create Reac app

⚔️ Install cypress

⚔️ Add a new script to package.json

⚔️ Check if cypress works

This opens Cypress Test Runner.

⚔️ Create a new test

Add a file named sample.spec.js in cypress/integration directory

⚔️ Restart cypress (yarn cy)

Click sample.spec.js (this runs the test)
(or yarn cypress run -spec ./cypress/integration/sample.spec.js)
You should be happy now 😃

⚔️ Edit App.js to make it counter app

Check if it works with yarn start

⚔️ Edit cypress.json to add url

⚔️ Add counter.spec.js in cypress/integration direactory

⚔️ Run test

Click counter.spec.js in Cypress Test Runner

🏴‍☠️Error with Cypress 4.5…

I encountered an error with Cypress 4.5. hmmm… 🤔 🤔 🤔

🔮 Ref

https://docs.cypress.io/guides/getting-started/installing-cypress.html#System-requirements

entry.title: Git: Merge only specific file
entry.content:
Git: Merge only specific file

I have created a branch(named Branch-B) from the master branch and then amended README.md file in the branch. However both master and Branch-B brances are needed to be kept and README.md needs to be updated in the master branch…

👍 Yay!

entry.title: Getting started with Mapbox
entry.content:
👑 Goal

Display map with Mapbox

🎓 Agenda

  1. Create Mapbox account
  2. Go to Mapbox studio
  3. Add Dataset
  4. Add Style
  5. Add map in markup and script

🚩 1. Create Mapbox account

  • https://www.mapbox.com/

🚩 2. Go to Mapbox studio

https://studio.mapbox.com/

🚩 3. Add Dataset

  • Create a dataset and Publish

🚩 4. Add Style

  • Select a theme –> Customize Basic
  • Add new layer and select the Dataset you created
  • Edit styles

  • Publish

🚩 5. Add map in markup and script

  • Mapbox example - Display a map with a custom style
    https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/custom-style-id/

  • Grab Style URL

  • Grab your Access Token form Account page

entry.title: Objectives at work
entry.content:
How to measure objectives

Follow the SMART template;

  1. Specific
  2. Measurable
  3. Attainable
  4. Relevant
  5. Time-Based
  • and SMART Goal

References

https://examples.yourdictionary.com/examples-of-measurable-goals-and-objectives.html

https://www.thebalancesmb.com/smart-goal-examples-2951827

entry.title: Splunk basics
entry.content:
Splunk basics

Tearms

Boolearns are casesensitive(upper case)

  • AND, OR, NOT

Constructing

Search Terms: host=hoe, error=500, user=*
Commands: table, rename, eval, chart/timechart, sort, stats

Timestamp

A Splunk generated default filed that represents time.
Format time

Generate table

DEMO chart for error 500:

DEMO stats and table for error 500

Add Comment to query

References

https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation
https://www.udemy.com/course/splunker/

entry.title: Getting started ES2015 and SASS(plus autoprefixer) with Webpack 4
entry.content:
👑 Goal

Create a ES2015 + Sass project with Webpack 4

🎓 Agenda

  1. Setup webpack
  2. Add HtmlWebpackPlugin
  3. Add webpack-dev-server
  4. Add Babel
  5. Add (SASS) SASS
  6. Add MiniCssExtractPlugin
  7. Add postcss-loader and autoprefixer

🚩 Step 1: Setup webpack

yarn init -y

yarn add -D webpack webpack-cli

👉 Create webpack.config.js

👉 Add scripts to package.json file

👉 Create src directory and add index.js file

✔️ TEST
yarn start ➡️ dist directory created with main.js file in it

💡 Git setup

git init

👉 Create .gitignore file

🚩 Step 2: HtmlWebpackPlugin

yarn add -D html-webpack-plugin

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

👉 Create template.html file in src directory

✔️ TEST
yarn start ➡️ index.html file created in dist directory

🚩 Step 3: Add webpack-dev-server

yarn add -D webpack-dev-server

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

👉 Edit package.json file

✔️ TEST
yarn start starts webpack-dev-server and the project is running at http://localhost:8080/

🚩 Step 4: Add Babel

yarn add -D babel-loader @babel/core @babel/preset-env

👉 Create .babelrc in root

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

👉 Add sub.js in src

👉 Edit index.js

✔️ TEST
yarn start –> open http://localhost:8080/ and hoe is logged in console.

🚩 Step 5: Add SASS(SCSS)

yarn add -D style-loader css-loader sass-loader node-sass

👉 Create style.scss in src

👉 Edit index.js

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

Add a new test in rules

✔️ TEST
yarn start –> open http://localhost:8080/ and background color is 🍅tomato.

🚩 Step 6: Add MiniCssExtractPlugin

yarn add -D mini-css-extract-plugin

👉 Remove style-loader

yarn remove style-loader

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

✔️ TEST
yarn build –> main.css is created in dist directory.

🚩 Step 7: Add Autoprefixer

yarn add -D postcss-loader and autoprefixer

👉 Create postcss.config.js in root

👉 Edit webpack.config.js

👉 Edit style.scss

✔️ TEST
yarn start –> open http://localhost:8080/ and <body/> has ‘display: -ms-grid’ and ‘display: grid’.

*This project can be found;

  • https://github.com/hoehoetester/hello-webpack4

entry.title: Angular EventEmitter
entry.content:
AngularのEventEmitter、なんだかずっとあまりよく理解できていなかった。

Reactの場合は、親から子へプロパティとして関数が渡されて、子がそれを呼び出す（callbackのように実行する）っていうふうに理解してたけど、
Angularの場合は子が関数を受け取るというよりも、その「関数を実行する」を発射するってカンジで、親がそれを検知して実行しているんだと思うと理解できた。

あぁ、だからAngularは「emit」なんだ。。。

ずーっともやもやしてたものが取れた気がする。これで少しはAngularが好きになれるかな。

entry.title: Angular - Content Projection
entry.content: Inject contents from parent component to child component to display the contents in ng-content tag inside the child component.

Step 2: Create index.html

entry.title: Order of css property with stylelint-config-idiomatic-css
entry.content:
1. Priority
  2. Positioning
  3. BoxModel
  4. Backgrounds
  5. Typography

1. Priority

  • content

2. Positioning

  • position
  • z-index
  • top
  • right
  • bottom
  • left

3. BoxModel

3.1 CSSGridLayoutModule

3.1.1 gridParentRules
  • grid-template
  • grid-template-columns
  • grid-template-rows
  • grid-template-areas
  • grid-gap
  • grid-column-gap
  • grid-row-gap
  • grid
  • grid-auto-columns
  • grid-auto-rows
  • grid-auto-flow
3.1.2 gridChildrenRules
  • grid-area
  • grid-column
  • grid-row
  • grid-column-start
  • grid-column-end
  • grid-row-start
  • grid-row-end

3.2 FlexibleBoxLayoutModule

  • flex
  • flex-grow
  • flex-shrink
  • flex-basis
  • flex-flow
  • flex-wrap
  • flex-direction
  • order

3.3 CSSBoxAlignmentModule

  • justify-items
  • justify-content
  • justify-self
  • align-items
  • align-content
  • align-self

#####

  • overflow
  • overflow-x
  • overflow-y
  • box-sizing
  • width
  • min-width
  • max-width
  • height
  • min-height
  • max-height
  • padding
  • padding-top
  • padding-right
  • padding-bottom
  • padding-left
  • border
  • border-top
  • border-right
  • border-bottom
  • border-left
  • border-width
  • border-top-width
  • border-right-width
  • border-bottom-width
  • border-left-width
  • margin
  • margin-top
  • margin-right
  • margin-bottom
  • margin-left

4. Backgrounds

4.1 other

  • background
  • background-color
  • background-image
  • background-position
  • background-size
  • background-repeat
  • background-origin
  • background-clip
  • background-attachment

5. Typography

  • color
  • font
  • font-family
  • font-size
  • font-style
  • font-variant
  • font-weight
  • line-height
  • letter-spacing
  • text-align
  • text-decoration
  • text-indent
  • text-overflow
  • text-shadow
  • text-transform
  • white-space
  • word-break
  • word-spacing

References

  • https://github.com/itslooklike/stylelint-config-idiomatic-css

entry.title: Angular - ng-template basic
entry.content:
What is ng-template?

ng-template creates templates that are not displayed by default.
It is displayed only when specified when used with a control structure such as if.

Demo

entry.title: Angular - ng-content basic
entry.content:
What is ng-content?

ng-content is a directive to get and display ContentChildren.
Use this when you want to inject an element into the component from outside.

Demo

entry.title: Getting started React with Express server
entry.content:
Goal

Create React app with express server

Step 1: Initialise package.json and add packages

Step 2: Create a server.js

Test

Open browser and go to http://localhost:1234/api/customers

Step 3: Create client with React

Step 4: Update package.json file both root and in client directory

Step 5: Update App.js to get data from server.js

Step 6: Run server and client

entry.title: Regex basic
entry.content:
Basics

  • . –> Any character (改行を除く任意の一文字)

To search meta characters, escape by a backslash.
(メタ文字自体を検索したい場合は”\“でエスケープする)

Character classes

  • \d –> 0-9
  • \w –> a-z, A-Z, 0-9, _
  • \s –> whitespace、tab、new line

(Present negations with uppercase)
(大文字にすると否定になる)

  • \D -> Not \d
  • \W -> Not \w
  • \S -> Not \s

Quantifiers

  • {n} –> n 個
  • {min,max} –> min 以上、max 以下
  • {min,} –> min 以上
  • {?} –> {0,1}
  • {+} –> {1,}
  • {\*} –> {0,}

03-3008-5432
0330085432
\d{2}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}

03-3008-5432
0330085432
TEL03-3008-5432
T0330085432
\w\*\d{2}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}

Anchors

  • ^ –> starts with
  • \$ –> ends with
  • –> Boundary 単語の境界

1 Tom 4
2 John 9
3 Hoe 3
4 HoeFua 8

  • ^\d –> only first number 最初の数字のみ
  • \d\$ –> only last number 末尾の数字のみ
  • –> 単語の境界を意味する Boundary
    Hoe –> only “Hoe”

OR operator

  • abc|123 -> “abc” or “123”

example.com
example.net

example\.com|example\.net
example\.(com|net)

Flags

  • /g –> Global
  • /i –> Insensitive
    /aBc/i would match “AbC”

Character Classes

  • [abc] –> “a” or “b” or “c”

bat hat cat

bat|hat|cat
(b|h|c)at
[bhc]at

Metacharacters Inside Character Classes

  • [^] -> NOT

bat hat cat eat
[^c]at –> Except for “cat”

Line breake and Tab

  • [t] –> Tab
  • [ ] –> Line breake（Windows）
  • [ ] –> Line breake（MacOS(~v9)）
  • [ ] –> Line breake（Unix, MacOS(v10~)）
  • [ | | ] –> Line breake（All OS）

Capture

  • ()
    • $1, $2, …

hoehoeblog, https://hoehoetester.github.io/
google, https://google.com
example, https://example.com

(.+),\s?(.+)
<a href="$2">\$1</a> –> <a href="https://hoehoetester.github.io/">hoehoeblog</a>

Reference

  • https://medium.com/factory-mind/regex-tutorial-a-simple-cheatsheet-by-examples-649dc1c3f285
  • https://qiita.com/Yametaro/items/36493c107053ae996b47

entry.title: Format Html query strings with VS Code
entry.content:

Add(Replace with) a new line with VS Code

Step 1: ☝️ Toggle on Use Regular Expression

Step2 : Enter regex to Search and Replace

entry.title: Getting started ES2015 with Parcel
entry.content:
Goal

Create a ES2015 project with Parcel

Step 1: Add package.json file and initialize it

Step 2: Add parcel

Step 3: Create a src directory and add files follow

  • index.html
  • script.js
  • style.scss

Step 4: Add scripts to package.json file

Step 5: Run scripts

yarn start

entry.title: Getting started ES2015 with Webpack
entry.content:
Goal

Create a ES2015 project with Webpack

Step 1: Add package.json file and initialize it

yarn init -y

Step 2: Add webpack

Add webpack modules follow

  • webpack
  • webpack-cli
  • webpack-dev-server

yarn add webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server -D

Step 3: Add scripts to package.json file

Step 4: Add ES2015

Add babel dev moduels follow

  • @babel/core
  • @babel/polyfill
  • @babel/preset-env

yarn add -D @babel/core @babel/polyfill @babel/preset-env

Create .babelrc file

Step 5: Add src directory then add index.js and helper.js files

Step 6: Create dist directory and add index.html file

Step 7: Run scripts

yarn start

*This project can be found;

  • https://github.com/hoehoetester/hello-es2015

References

  • https://webpack.js.org/guides/getting-started/

entry.title: Hexo deployment settings
entry.content:
*This deploying sample is for Github

  1. Add hexo-deployer-git
    yarn add hexo-deployer-git
  1. Add the settings follows to the root _config.yml.

References

  • https://github.com/hexojs/hexo-deployer-git

entry.title: aria-label vs aria-labelledby vs aria-describedby
entry.content:
aria-label

  • This provides a recognizable name for the object to the user.
  • If the label text is visible on screen, authors SHOULD use aria-labelledby and SHOULD NOT use aria-label. Use aria-label only if the interface is such that it is not possible to have a visible label on the screen.
  • The aria-label is used to add an accessible name directly to an element.


aria-labelledby

  • This provides a recognizable name for the object to the user.
  • If the label text is visible on screen, authors SHOULD use aria-labelledby and SHOULD NOT use aria-label.


–> Santa Claus is laughing.



–> Ho ho ho ho ho ho



aria-describedby

  • This is for the purpose of providing more detailed information




Summary

  • The aria-label is used to add an accessible name directly to an element.
  • aria-labelledby can be specified multiple ids
  • Is the label text visible?
    • YES: aria-labelledby
    • NO: aria-label
  • When explaining in:
    • Short label(s)：aria-labelledby
    • Sentences：aria-describedby



References

  • https://www.w3.org/TR/2014/REC-wai-aria-20140320/states_and_properties
  • https://www.w3.org/WAI/tips/developing/#include-alternative-text-for-images
  • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Accessibility/HTML?document_saved=true#Text_alternatives



tabindex

  • tabindex=”int”: Moving order
  • tabindex=”0”: Focusable
  • tabindex=”-1”: Unfocusable

entry.title: Jest basic
entry.content:
Setup Jest: Getting started Jest with Typescript

toBe() vs toEqual()

toBe compares strict equality, using ===.
toEqual compares the values of two variables. If it’s an object or array, it checks the equality of all the properties or elements.

Run only the test group

Skip the test group

Loop tests with multiple test cases

Run only fit tests

Skip only xit tests

References

  • https://flaviocopes.com/jest/#introduction-to-jest

entry.title: Getting started Jest with Typescript
entry.content:
Goal

Write unit test with Jest and Typescript

Agenda

  1. Step 1: Simplest start set
  2. Step 2: Add Babel for ES2015
  3. Step 3: Add Typescript

* Each steps can be seen in Github brances
https://github.com/hoehoetester/hello-jest

Step 1: Simplest start set

Create a directory and install jest

Create a js file to be tested named sum.js

Create a test js file to test named sum.test.js

Add scripts to package.json

Open terminal and run test

Step 2: Add ES2015

Add babel dev moduels follow

  • @babel/core
  • @babel/polyfill
  • @babel/preset-env

Create a .babelrc file

Updates js files with ES2015 syntax

Step 3: Add Typescript

Add typescript dev moduels follow

  • @babel/preset-typescript
  • @types/jest
  • babel-jest

Update .babelrc file

Change js files to typescritp files

  • sum.js –> sum.ts
  • sum.test.ts –> sum.test.ts

Update sum.ts files with typescript

References

  • https://jestjs.io/docs/en/getting-started

entry.title: How to write draft post in Hexo
entry.content:
1. Create a new draft post
    hexo new draft "Hexo how to write draft post"

  2. Preview draft on your local server
    hexo server --draft

  3. Publish the draft post
    hexo publish "Hexo how to write draft post"

entry.title: Markdown on Github
entry.content:
Markdown basics

https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown

GitHub supports emoji!

Emoji cheat sheet

entry.title: Git commit user setting
entry.content:
Change(or add) name and email in config file to change the commit user.

/.git/config

entry.title: Awesome Javascript websites
entry.content:
Tutorial

  • https://javascript.info/
  • https://www.codingame.com/learn

Other

  • http://jsben.ch/
  • http://placekitten.com/
  • https://reqres.in/
  • https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/

entry.content:
WAI-ARIA_basics(Japanese)

Slide(Japanese)

aria-label vs aria-labelledby vs aria-describedby

Tips for Getting Started Developing for Web Accessibility
